{{Quote|Gol toq!|Unda Lagor (loosely translated as "Blast them!")|<ref name="tdc" />}}
'''Unda Lagor''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Quara/Legends|Quara]] [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] who enjoyed violence, murder, and destruction. After serving with a [[Unidentified mercenary group|mercenary group]], he went on a wild [[Death/Legends|killing]] spree through a [[Kal'Shebbol starport|starport]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Massacring civilians and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] alike, Lagor was eventually [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] by the [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] of the planet's ruler, Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], and kept in the detention cells of the [[Imperial Government Complex|Imperial headquarters]].

Freed during the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|assault]] on the planet in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Lagor subsequently joined the crew of the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' when they began recruiting from the planet's civilian population due to a manpower shortage. The New Republic had tasked the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] with [[DarkStryder Campaign|tracking down]] Sarne, who had fled his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] following the liberation of Kal'Shebbol. Lagor, having developed a hatred for the Moff and Imperials in general, claimed he was a [[Politics/Legends|political]] [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] and was assigned as a [[Heavy weapon specialist|heavy weapons specialist]]. He kept quiet about the reason for his incarceration, fearing that if the truth was known he would be ejected from the ship via an [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]]. Lagor remained aboard the vessel until it reached the planet [[Kathol]], where Sarne was finally defeated in a decisive [[Battle of Kathol System|battle]].

==Biography==
Unda Lagor, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]]<ref name="tdc" /> of the [[Quara/Legends|Quara]] [[Subspecies/Legends|subspecies]],<ref name="Visual" /> served as a member of a renegade [[Unidentified mercenary group|mercenary group]], and, during his [[Time/Legends|time]] with them, he gained experience with [[Repeating blaster/Legends|repeating blasters]] and [[blaster artillery]]. He eventually left the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and found himself on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] around [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. Kal'Shebbol was part of the dominion of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]]<ref name="tdc" /> who had seceded from the Empire following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="tsb">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> Sometime prior to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] of the planet in 8 ABY, Lagor went on a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] rampage through one of the busiest sectors of Kal'Shebbol's [[Kal'Shebbol starport|starport]]. The Aqualish ransacked several buildings and [[Death/Legends|killed]] forty-three civilians and twenty-one Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|troops]] before he was finally captured and incarcerated in the [[Prison/Legends|dungeons]] of Sarne's [[Imperial Government Complex|headquarters]]. His excuse for his actions was that he was simply having a bad day.<ref name="tdc" />

When the New Republic liberated Kal'Shebbol, elements of [[Katarn Commandos|Page's Commandos]] released [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] from the dungeons during the battle while searching for a New Republic operative, a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] by the name of [[Lofryyhn]].<ref name="tsb" /> Freed from his cell, Lagor escaped the facility, pausing only to acquire the [[Blast helmet/Legends|blast helmet]] and [[Blast vest/Legends|vest]] from the dead body of an [[Unidentified Imperial soldier (Kal'Shebbol)|Imperial trooper]], one of many whom Sarne left behind to defend his palace while the Moff fled the planet.<ref name="tdc" /> With Sarne ousted from Kal'Shebbol, the New Republic authorized the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' to travel into the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]] to [[DarkStryder Campaign|track down]] the rogue Moff.<ref name="tsb" /> However, due to a lack of available manpower, the New Republic was forced to recruit from Kal'Shebbol's civilian population to crew the [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. Eager to get even with the warlord, Lagor signed up for the mission by claiming to have been one of the Moff's [[Politics/Legends|political]] prisoners. He was accepted and assigned as one of the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette's]] [[Heavy weapon specialist|heavy weapons specialists]]. During the [[Standard month/Legends|months]]-long mission, Lagor had the habit of trying to get himself assigned to ground missions as "firepower support" so that he could shoot at something.<ref name="tdc" />

Due to his imprisonment, Lagor had developed a hatred for Sarne and, by extension, all Imperials. Many of the crew feared that he would focus his wrath on [[Jessa Dajus]], a former member of Sarne's [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] who was part of the corvette's command staff, as she openly wore her [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|Imperial uniform]] while on duty. However, the Aqualish was afraid that Dajus knew the real reason he had been in the detention cells, and did not want that information spreading to the rest of the crew for fear that they would eject him from an [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]]. As a result, he deliberately avoided Dajus.<ref name="tdc" />

As the ''FarStar'' neared the end of its mission, the crew landed on the planet [[Kathol]]. While attempting to rally the native [[Yapi]] against the combined might of Sarne's Imperial forces and the [[DarkStryder]], a [[Bioengineering/Legends|bio-engineered]] lifeform ruling the planet, members of the crew were invited to take a [[Test]] to prove their worth. The Test consisted of six jars—five empty and one containing a captive [[Maoi]]—placed in a ten-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-deep pit. The candidate, armed only with a [[Club/Legends|club]], had to smash at least two of the jars without releasing the Maoi, an amorphous lifeform that attacked prey by assaulting the face, then slithering down the assailant's [[Throat/Legends|throat]] to devour their internal organs. If the Maoi was released, then the candidate failed the Test and usually died from the Maoi's attack unless they were able to escape or subdue the creature. Lagor was one of several crew members who was considered as a potential candidate to take the Test to secure the assistance of the Yapi in the subsequent [[Battle of Kathol System|battle]] that saw the end of both Sarne and the DarkStryder.<ref name="e">''[[Endgame]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
An Aqualish, Unda Lagor<ref name="tdc" /> was a member of the Quara [[Species/Legends|subspecies]], possessing five-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]].<ref name="Visual" /> Lagor had [[Color/Legends|tan]] skin, [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and tufts of white facial [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. He was an antagonistic individual who took pleasure in inciting violence or murdering others and was often irritable if he had not "done good death." Following his imprisonment in Moff Sarne's dungeons, Lagor harbored a vengeance against Sarne and, by extension, any other Imperial he encountered. The Aqualish used bullying tactics to get his own way, although he was afraid that his targets would join forces and seek their revenge on him.<ref name="tdc" />

==Equipment==
Lagor was never far away from his preferred weapon, a [[Light repeating blaster/Legends|light repeating blaster]], and often carried it slung over his shoulder. However, he was fond of turning around and whacking others with it, seemingly by accident. He was also trained in blaster [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]], vehicle-mounted weapons, and [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee combat]]. He could drive [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles. During Lagor's assignment on the ''FarStar'', he wore the blast [[Armor/Legends|helmet]] and vest that he had taken from the corpse of a dead Imperial soldier during his escape from the detention cells.<ref name="tdc" />

==Behind the scenes==
Unda Lagor was created for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a multi-book adventure for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[West End Games]]. Lagor is a member of the support crew of the ''FarStar'', and the gamemaster and players are given free reign to develop the Aqualish's character during the adventure if they so wish, since the designers did not plan to include him in future supplements. Despite this, Lagor did receive a mention in the final book of the campaign, ''[[Endgame]]''. His entry was written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], and his illustration was provided by [[Mike Vilardi]].
